seoTitle: Portsmith vs Poruma Island — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Portsmith and Poruma Island. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Portsmith vs Poruma Island: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Portsmith and Poruma Island.
heading: Portsmith vs Poruma Island — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Portsmith, Queensland and Poruma Island, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Portsmith has a lower overall climate risk score (27/100) compared to Poruma Island (33/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Portsmith or Poruma Island safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Portsmith has a flood risk score of 23/100, while Poruma Island scores 35/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Portsmith scores 29/100 for bushfire risk. Poruma Island scores 38/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Portsmith or Poruma Island?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Portsmith has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.4% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
